name: clamp
description: The standalone lodash.clamp package as a vendorable file. Useful when you want a tiny numeric helper without adding another dependency.
tagline: Constrain a number between lower and upper bounds.
summary: This page ships the published lodash.clamp file in both raw and normalized forms. The value is to keep a known tiny helper local when you do not want to install it.
nativeAlternative: Math.min and Math.max cover most of the idea, but vendoring keeps the exact existing helper contract intact.

variants.label: Normalized
variants.note: Rule-based normalization for modern TS projects. The goal is easier import and build compatibility, not changed behavior.

variants.label: Raw
variants.note: Published package file kept as-is for older teams or repos that still prefer the original CommonJS distribution.

note: Raw and normalized views are both available. Use normalized when you want an ESM and TypeScript-friendly file, and raw when you want the original published package shape.
